title: What three weeks teaches that one cannot.
dek: On the slow arithmetic of long trips, and why a fortnight is never quite enough.
author: Pamela Sarti
body:
[PLACEHOLDER — to be written]

The first week is logistics. The second is adjustment. The third is when the trip actually begins.

## The arithmetic of long trips

[PLACEHOLDER — to be written]

> A short trip rewards the version of you that left home. A long one rewards the version that arrives.

title: Why we don't list every island.
dek: A short defense of editing, in a discipline that rewards exhaustiveness.
author: Pamela Sarti
body:
[PLACEHOLDER — to be written]

There are six hundred kite spots on the map. There are not six hundred trips.

## What gets cut

[PLACEHOLDER — to be written]

> The list is shorter than it could be on purpose. That is the work.

## What stays

title: On the body deciding before you do.
dek: Notes on fear, fatigue, and the older intelligence that picks the line through.
author: Pamela Sarti
body:
[PLACEHOLDER — to be written]

The foot lands before the thought arrives. The hand finds the hold before the eye approves it.

## Before the mind

[PLACEHOLDER — to be written]

> You don't choose the line. You recognize it.
